executive_summary.one_paragraph: CLDN18 (claudin-18) is a four-pass tight-junction protein with two isoforms: CLDN18.1 (lung) and CLDN18.2 (stomach/tumors). Surface accessibility is established by live-cell flow cytometry, membranous IHC across multiple tumor cohorts, 89Zr-zolbetuximab PET imaging in xenografts, surfaceome mass spectrometry, and CAR-T on-target clinical toxicity. Zolbetuximab (anti-CLDN18.2) is FDA-approved for gastric/GEJ adenocarcinoma. Key caveats: junctional subdomain restriction in normal epithelium (partially relieved in tumors), isoform selectivity requirements, and uncertain surface retention in CLDN18-ARHGAP26 fusion contexts.

surface_evidence.grade_rationale: CLDN18 surface accessibility is supported by multiple independent direct methods from multiple sources. (1) Live-cell flow cytometry (non-permeabilized): Sahin et al. 2008 (PMID:19047087) demonstrated isoform-specific antibody binding to cancer cell surfaces; PMC11712040 confirmed surface expression of both isoforms on N87 cells via live FACS; PMC12323529 and PMC12808354 provide additional live-cell FACS methodological anchors. (2) IHC with explicit membranous scoring (cytoplasmic excluded): multiple primary tumor cohorts including BTC (PMC12018027), PDAC (PMC11979426), and others document membranous CLDN18 staining. (3) In vivo functional antibody engagement: 89Zr-zolbetuximab PET imaging in xenografts (PMC12766881) showed specific tumor uptake far exceeding IgG control, confirming circulating-antibody-accessible surface epitope. (4) CAR-T on-target toxicity in gastric mucosa (PMC12579600) provides clinical human in vivo functional evidence of surface exposure. (5) Surfaceome mass spectrometry (PMC6528836) identified CLDN18 with extracellular antigenic epitopes. Collectively, live-cell flow cytometry, membranous IHC, in vivo antibody PET imaging, and functional CAR-T engagement constitute well-replicated multi-method direct surface evidence from independent sources.

antibodies.name: anti-CLDN18.2 monoclonal antibody (isoform-specific)
antibodies.cross_reactivity_notes: Demonstrated to bind CLDN18.2 but not CLDN18.1 (lung-specific splice variant); isoform selectivity confirmed in the original 2008 Sahin et al. study.

expression_observations.context: Cancer cell lines (unspecified) expressing CLDN18.2; antibody binds antigen on the surface of cancer cells

antibodies.name: pan-specific anti-CLDN18 antibody (recognizing CLDN18.1 and CLDN18.2)
antibodies.cross_reactivity_notes: Pan-specific; recognizes both isoforms (CLDN18.1 and CLDN18.2); used on cells ectopically expressing each isoform.

expression_observations.context: N87 gastric cancer cells ectopically expressing human CLDN18.1 and CLDN18.2; surface expression of both isoforms confirmed by flow cytometry

antibodies.name: anti-CLDN18.2 test antibodies (serial dilution cell binding assay)

expression_observations.context: CLDN18.2-expressing target cancer cell lines; serial dilution antibody binding assay with live/dead gating
CLDN18.2-expressing cancer cells; live-cell FACS used to directly measure antibody binding to cell-surface CLDN18.2

antibodies.name: anti-CLDN18 antibody (SPOTLIGHT/GLOW trial-aligned IHC)
antibodies.cross_reactivity_notes: Scoring criterion: ≥75% of tumor cells with moderate-to-strong membranous staining; cytoplasmic staining excluded from positivity.

expression_observations.context: Biliary tract cancer (BTC) primary tumor tissue; 237 samples; CLDN18 expression in 29.5% of cases; CLDN18 positivity (≥75% moderate-to-strong membranous) in 5.5% of BTCs

antibodies.name: anti-CLDN18 antibody clone 43-14A
antibodies.cross_reactivity_notes: IHC scoring for 1+ vs. 2+ membranous staining intensity described as not well-defined; guideline ambiguity noted in PDAC cohort context.

expression_observations.context: Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (PDAC) primary tumor tissue; cases with 2+ or 3+ membranous staining in 40–75% range identified; scoring ambiguity for 1+ vs. 2+ intensity noted
Gastric/gastroesophageal junction adenocarcinoma; clinical trial eligibility required ≥75% tumor cells with moderate-to-strong membranous CLDN18 IHC staining (SPOTLIGHT and GLOW trials)

antibodies.name: anti-CLDN18 antibody (membranous staining only; cytoplasmic excluded)
antibodies.cross_reactivity_notes: Only membranous staining counted as positive; cytoplasmic staining explicitly excluded.

antibodies.name: anti-CLDN18 antibody (membranous staining quantitative scoring system)
antibodies.cross_reactivity_notes: Scored on 0–5+ scale based on percentage of tumor cells with any membranous staining.

expression_observations.context: Ovarian tumor primary tissue vs. normal tissue; CLDN18 identified among adhesion proteins with extracellular antigenic epitopes showing highest differential expression (alongside EpCAM and CLDN1); Cell Surface Protein Atlas (CSPA) mass spectrometry-based annotation

antibodies.name: 89Zr-labeled zolbetuximab (anti-CLDN18.2 monoclonal antibody)
antibodies.cross_reactivity_notes: Specificity confirmed by comparison to [89Zr]Zr-DFO-IgG isotype control; tumor-specific uptake significantly higher than control (P=0.0029 in GSU model).

expression_observations.context: GSU (gastric cancer) xenograft; day 6 mean tumor uptake: 24.4 ± 7.8 %ID/g vs. IgG control 4.8 ± 1.9 %ID/g (P=0.0029)
PATU8988S (pancreatic cancer) xenograft; day 6 mean tumor uptake: 36.6 ± 10.1 %ID/g
HUPT-4 (pancreatic cancer) xenograft; day 6 mean tumor uptake: 16.48 ± 4.7 %ID/g

antibodies.name: CLDN18.2-targeted CAR-T cell receptor (therapeutic engagement)
antibodies.cross_reactivity_notes: On-target off-tumor toxicity in gastric mucosa confirms surface accessibility of CLDN18.2 in normal gastric epithelium.

expression_observations.context: Gastric cancer patients treated with CLDN18.2-targeted CAR-T therapy; promising clinical efficacy reported in early-phase trials
Normal gastric mucosa; on-target off-tumor recognition of CLDN18.2 by CAR-T cells causing gastrointestinal adverse events in early-phase trials, confirming surface accessibility in normal epithelium

antibodies.name: Zolbetuximab (anti-CLDN18.2 monoclonal antibody, review context)
antibodies.cross_reactivity_notes: In CLDN18-ARHGAP26 fusion protein context, surface localization of the fusion protein requires further confirmation; standard CLDN18.2 surface accessibility may not apply.

expression_observations.context: Tumors with CLDN18-ARHGAP26 fusion protein; surface localization of fusion protein uncertain; additional localization studies required

non_surface_expression.context: Human PDAC tumor tissue vs. noncancerous pancreas tissue; RNA sequencing showed significantly higher CLDN18.2 expression in PDAC with no significant difference between treated and untreated tumors
CLDN18 IHC membranous staining in 237 biliary tract cancer samples; 29.5% showed any expression, 5.5% met positivity threshold (≥75% cells with moderate-to-strong membranous staining)
CLDN18 membranous IHC in pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma cohort using clone 43-14A; cases with 2+ or 3+ staining in 40-75% range noted
PDAC imaging target evaluation: CLDN18 listed as membrane-localized protein with tumor expression 64.4 vs. normal 133.3 (ratio 2.1) from database/transcriptomic annotation

therapeutic_engagement.description: Zolbetuximab, an anti-CLDN18.2 monoclonal antibody, received regulatory approval for gastric/gastroesophageal junction adenocarcinoma (SPOTLIGHT and GLOW phase III trials), establishing CLDN18.2 as a validated therapeutic target. CLDN18.2-targeted CAR-T cell therapies have shown promising efficacy in early-phase gastric cancer trials. Preclinical in vivo work includes 89Zr- and 177Lu-labeled zolbetuximab for PET imaging and radioligand therapy in PDAC and gastric cancer xenograft models. Multiple biologic programs (antibodies, CAR-T) are documented across gastric cancer, PDAC, and biliary tract cancers.
therapeutic_engagement.surface_form_rationale: CLDN18 is a four-pass transmembrane tight-junction protein with no known secreted or shed isoform. Therapeutics engage the membrane-anchored form exclusively, accessing extracellular loop epitopes on intact tumor cell surfaces. This is confirmed by live-cell flow cytometry (isoforms CLDN18.1 and CLDN18.2), in vivo PET imaging showing specific tumor uptake of radiolabeled zolbetuximab, and on-target off-tumor CAR-T toxicity in normal gastric mucosa.

anatomical_accessibility.rationale: CLDN18 constitutes tight junction strands at cell-cell contacts in polarized epithelium. Tight junction–restricted localization is physically occluded by adjacent cells; systemic antibodies have limited access to the junctional membrane subdomain under normal barrier-intact conditions.

CLDN18.2 is a tight junction protein in differentiated gastric epithelial cells facing the gastric lumen. Its junctional localization in intact normal gastric mucosa limits access by systemically delivered binders, as the epithelial barrier and cell-cell contacts shield the junction.

Malignant transformation disrupts normal tight junction architecture, potentially relocating CLDN18.2 to more accessible membrane surfaces. Retained surface expression in gastric tumors and metastases suggests improved systemic accessibility vs. normal mucosa, but residual junctional trapping is possible.

CLDN18.1 localizes to tight junctions of alveolar epithelial cells. The air-blood barrier formed by alveolar tight junctions limits systemic antibody access to the junctional domain from the blood side; the apical/luminal surface faces the airspace.

Surfaceome and IHC data confirm membranous CLDN18 in PDAC with ~2-fold tumor enrichment vs. normal pancreas. Disrupted epithelial polarity in PDAC may redistribute CLDN18 from tight junctions to more accessible lateral/basolateral membrane, but precise orientation in tumor context is unresolved.

accessibility_modulation.modulating_state: Differentiated gastric mucosal epithelial cells (normal stomach)
accessibility_modulation.change: CLDN18.2 isoform is strictly confined to differentiated gastric epithelial cells (gastrocytes) in normal tissue, absent from all other normal non-gastric lineages and absent from the gastric stem cell zone.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: CLDN18.2 surface epitopes are accessible only at the tight junctions of differentiated gastric epithelium in normal tissue, making it a highly lineage-selective surface target with minimal off-tumor risk in non-gastric normal tissues.

accessibility_modulation.modulating_state: Alveolar epithelium of the normal lung
accessibility_modulation.change: CLDN18.1 (lung-specific isoform) is expressed selectively in alveolar epithelial cells (pneumocytes) as a tight junction component, driven by NKX2.1 transcriptional control; absent from non-lung lineages.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: CLDN18.1 surface accessibility is restricted to lung alveolar epithelium; binders targeting CLDN18.1 will engage only this epithelial compartment in normal tissue.

accessibility_modulation.baseline_context: Gastric stem cell zone (undifferentiated gastric progenitor cells)
accessibility_modulation.change: CLDN18.2 surface expression is absent in the gastric stem cell zone and appears only upon differentiation of gastric epithelial cells, indicating a differentiation-stage gate on surface expression.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: Therapeutic binders to CLDN18.2 will not engage gastric stem/progenitor cells, reducing risk of targeting the regenerative compartment; surface access is restricted to the differentiated epithelial pool.

accessibility_modulation.baseline_context: Normal gastric mucosa (differentiated epithelial cells expressing CLDN18.2 at tight junctions)
accessibility_modulation.modulating_state: Primary gastric cancer and gastric cancer metastases
accessibility_modulation.change: CLDN18.2 surface expression is retained upon malignant transformation and is present in a significant proportion of primary gastric cancers and their metastases, maintaining membranous localization.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: CLDN18.2 remains accessible at the cancer cell surface in gastric tumors and metastases, supporting its utility as a therapeutic target (e.g., zolbetuximab) across primary and metastatic disease settings.

accessibility_modulation.baseline_context: Normal pancreatic, esophageal, ovarian, and lung tissues (no CLDN18.2 expression)
accessibility_modulation.modulating_state: Pancreatic, esophageal, ovarian, and lung tumors (ectopic CLDN18.2 activation)
accessibility_modulation.change: CLDN18.2 is ectopically activated in pancreatic (~20.7%), esophageal, ovarian, and lung mucinous adenocarcinoma (~68%) tumors not normally expressing this isoform, with CLDN18.2 predominating over CLDN18.1 in pan-cancer tumor samples (mean proportion 0.945).
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: Ectopic CLDN18.2 surface expression in these tumor types creates neo-accessible epitopes absent from corresponding normal tissues, broadening the therapeutic window for CLDN18.2-directed agents beyond gastric cancer.

accessibility_modulation.baseline_context: Normal pancreatic tissue (low CLDN18 surface expression)
accessibility_modulation.change: Surfaceome proteomics shows CLDN18 membrane levels are ~2.1-fold higher in PDAC tumor versus normal pancreatic tissue; IHC confirms membranous expression in a subset of PDAC with moderate-to-strong staining in ≥75% of cells in some cases.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: CLDN18 is selectively enriched at the PDAC tumor cell surface relative to normal pancreas, with low expression in most toxicity-prone normal tissues, supporting a favorable therapeutic index for CLDN18-targeted therapy in PDAC.

accessibility_modulation.baseline_context: Normal biliary tract / bile duct epithelium
accessibility_modulation.modulating_state: Biliary tract cancer (BTC), cholangiocarcinoma, and biliary precursor lesions (IPNB, epithelial dysplasia)
accessibility_modulation.change: CLDN18 protein is expressed in 29.5% of BTC samples (membranous), CLDN18.2 in ~52% of cholangiocarcinomas, and is detectable in biliary precursor lesions including IPNB and dysplastic epithelium adjacent to cholangiocarcinoma.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: CLDN18.2 surface epitopes become accessible early in biliary carcinogenesis and persist in frank BTC, providing a targetable surface antigen across a spectrum of biliary disease states, though at lower frequency than gastric cancer.

accessibility_modulation.change: CLDN18.2 is ectopically expressed in moderately differentiated gastric-type cervical adenocarcinomas, correlating with a gastric differentiation phenotype rather than CLDN18 gene rearrangement.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: CLDN18.2 surface epitopes become accessible in a subset of cervical adenocarcinomas with gastric-type differentiation, expanding the potential patient population for CLDN18.2-directed therapy to this gynecological tumor subtype.

accessibility_modulation.baseline_context: Normal alveolar epithelium (CLDN18.1 expressed at tight junctions)
accessibility_modulation.change: CLDN18 (CLDN18.1) expression is nearly completely lost in NM-LUAD: absent in 83% of resections and 84.8% of biopsies, including 24/26 lepidic component cases, contrasting with robust expression in adjacent normal lung.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: CLDN18.1 surface accessibility is dramatically reduced in NM-LUAD, making CLDN18-directed therapies poorly suited for this lung cancer subtype; binders would find minimal target on NM-LUAD cells.

accessibility_modulation.modulating_state: Adenocarcinoma of the esophagogastric junction (AEG) and Barrett's-associated neoplasia (CD-SBN) with gastric-type differentiation
accessibility_modulation.change: CLDN18 is enriched (positive log2 fold-change) at the tumor cell surface in AEG versus paired normal adjacent tissue by surfaceome proteomics; ~91% of CD-SBN lesions show moderate/strong membranous CLDN18 expression, often co-expressed with gastric mucins.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: CLDN18 surface epitopes are selectively accessible in GEJ adenocarcinoma and gastric-type Barrett's neoplasia, establishing these disease states as contexts where CLDN18-directed binders can engage tumor cell membranes.

accessibility_modulation.baseline_context: Gastric cancer cells expressing wild-type CLDN18.2 at the cell surface
accessibility_modulation.modulating_state: Gastric cancer cells harboring CLDN18-ARHGAP26 gene fusion
accessibility_modulation.change: The CLDN18-ARHGAP26 fusion protein has uncertain cellular localization; it is unknown whether sufficient CLDN18 epitopes remain at the cell surface versus being redistributed intracellularly due to the fusion partner.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: If the fusion redirects CLDN18 away from the plasma membrane, surface accessibility for antibody- or CAR-T-based therapies could be compromised in fusion-positive gastric tumors, requiring additional localization studies before targeting.

surface_bind.attribution: © Marchand, Khakzad, Correia et al. — EPFL / Inria / Novo Nordisk

co_receptor_requirements.rationale: CLDN18 is an intrinsic four-pass transmembrane tight-junction protein that traffics autonomously to the plasma membrane. No obligate co-receptor or chaperone for surface expression is documented in the merged ledger. Live-cell flow cytometry confirms surface expression of both isoforms independently when ectopically expressed in N87 cells.

restricted_subdomain.rationale: CLDN18 constitutes tight-junction strands at cell-cell contacts in polarized epithelium (HPA annotation; NKX2.1-regulated lung epithelium; differentiated gastric mucosa). In normal tissues, junctional localization physically restricts systemic antibody access. In tumors, disrupted polarity partially relocates CLDN18.2 to more accessible lateral membrane, but residual junctional trapping is possible. CAR-T on-target toxicity and zolbetuximab PET imaging confirm functional access in tumor and normal gastric mucosa contexts.

ecd_size_assessment.rationale: CLDN18 is a four-pass claudin with two small extracellular loops (ECL1 ~50 aa, ECL2 ~20 aa). Despite limited ECD, the extracellular loops are functionally accessible: isoform-specific antibodies (zolbetuximab), CAR-T receptors, and PET-imaging constructs all successfully engage the ECL epitopes on intact tumor cells.

epitope_masking.rationale: In intact tight junctions, CLDN18 ECLs are partly occluded by adjacent claudin-claudin trans-interactions and junctional complex architecture, reducing epitope exposure to circulating binders. Tumor-associated disruption of tight junction integrity is the primary mechanism relieving this masking. The CLDN18-ARHGAP26 fusion represents an additional conformational uncertainty where surface epitope presentation may be altered. Glycan-mediated masking is not documented in the ledger.

evidence.claim: Monoclonal antibodies raised against CLDN18.2 (the gastric isoform) specifically bind CLDN18.2 but not the lung-specific splice variant CLDN18.1, and recognize the antigen on the surface of cancer cells. This foundational 2008 study by Sahin et al. established CLDN18.2 as a pan-cancer surface target and demonstrated cell-surface accessibility of the extracellular epitope by antibody binding.

spans.quote:
Most importantly, we were able to raise monoclonal antibodies that bind to CLDN18.2 but not to its lung-specific splice variant and recognize the antigen on the surface of cancer cells.

Conclusions

Its highly restricted expression pattern in normal tissues, its frequent ectopic activation in a diversity of human cancers, and the ability to specifically target this molecule at the cell surface of tumor cells qualify CLDN18.2 as a novel, highly attractive pan-cancer target for the antibody therapy of epithelial tumors.

evidence.claim: Flow cytometry analysis on live N87 gastric cancer cells ectopically expressing either CLDN18.1 or CLDN18.2 using a pan-specific anti-CLDN18 antibody (recognizing both isoforms) demonstrated surface expression of both CLDN18.1 and CLDN18.2. This is a direct surface-expression measurement using intact live cells with no permeabilization, confirming plasma-membrane accessibility of both isoforms.

spans.quote: Left, Flow cytometry analysis of CLDN18.1 and CLDN18.2 surface expression in N87 cells ectopically expressing human CLDN18.1 and CLDN18.2 using a pan-specific anti-CLDN18 antibody recognizing both isoforms.

evidence.claim: Cell binding assay (FACS) on target cells using serial antibody dilutions (1 mM start, 4-fold dilution across 10 points) with secondary antibody (Thermo Fisher Scientific Cat# A-21445, RRID:AB_2535862) and live/dead red fixable reagent gating. This methodological detail anchors the flow-cytometry surface-binding assay for CLDN18.2-targeting antibodies, specifying buffer conditions, antibody RRID, and live-cell gating strategy.

spans.quote: For cell binding assays, serial dilutions of test and control antibodies were incubated with target cells in FACS staining buffer, starting at a concentration of 1 mM and diluted fourfold across 10 points, followed by washing with FACS staining buffer and staining with secondary antibody (Thermo Fisher Scientific Cat# A-21445, RRID:AB_2535862) at a final concentration of 4 ug/mL along with live/dead red fixable reagent (ThermoFisher) at 1/1000 in FACS staining buffer for 1 hour at 4°C.

evidence.claim: FACS on live cells was used to directly measure antibody binding to cell-surface CLDN18.2 protein on the cell membrane. This surface-detection method statement confirms that live-cell FACS is the assay employed to screen antibodies specifically targeting CLDN18.2 at the cell surface, without permeabilization.

spans.quote: FACS allows us to directly measure the binding of antibodies to cell surface proteins, which is highly relevant to our goal of screening antibodies that specifically target CLAUDIN 18.2 on the cell membrane.

evidence.claim: IHC scoring methodology for CLDN18 membranous staining in tumor tissue: positivity defined as ≥75% of tumor cells with moderate-to-strong membranous staining, following the evaluation methods used in the SPOTLIGHT and GLOW phase III trials. This operationalizes surface detection of CLDN18 by IHC for clinical trial-aligned assessments.

spans.quote:
Tumor positivity for CLDN18 was determined if ≥75% of tumor cells exhibited moderate-to-strong membranous staining, following the evaluation methods used in the SPOTLIGHT and GLOW trials. 10 , 11 , 18
The presence and pattern of intratumoral heterogeneity was assessed only in cases with CLDN18 positivity, as weak staining makes it difficult to assess staining patterns.

evidence.claim: IHC homogeneity scoring criterion: absence of intratumoral heterogeneity defined as CLDN18 expressed in >90% of tumor area with moderate-to-strong membranous staining pattern. This method detail specifies how membranous surface staining is quantified for CLDN18.

spans.quote: The absence of intratumoral heterogeneity (i.e. homogenous pattern of expression of CLDN18) was defined as CLDN18 being expressed in >90% of the area with a moderate-to-strong membranous staining.

evidence.claim: IHC of 237 biliary tract cancer (BTC) samples showed CLDN18 expression in 29.5% of cases overall, with CLDN18 positivity (moderate-to-strong membranous staining in ≥75% of tumor cells) in 5.5% of BTCs. This result demonstrates membranous surface localization of CLDN18 in BTC tumor tissue by IHC.

spans.quote: Overall, CLDN18 expression was observed in 70/237 BTCs (29.5%) samples, while CLDN18 positivity (i.e. moderate-to-strong membranous expression in ≥75% of the tumor cells) was found in 13/237 BTCs (5.5%).

evidence.claim: IHC with CLDN18 clone 43-14A in a PDAC cohort showed cases with 2+ or 3+ membranous staining in the 40–75% range required careful scoring consideration. This result documents moderate-to-strong membranous CLDN18 surface staining in pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, quantified by IHC with the 43-14A antibody clone.

spans.quote: With CLDN18/43–14A staining in our PDAC cohort, we found that cases exhibiting 2+ or 3+ membranous staining in the 40–75 % range required more careful consideration for scoring.

evidence.claim: IHC scoring of CLDN18 membranous staining using the 43-14A clone is acknowledged to have poorly described guidelines distinguishing 1+ vs. 2+ staining intensity, representing a methodological ambiguity in interpreting surface-detection results. This caveat should be carried as a methodological limitation.

spans.quote: Another challenge with the pathologic interpretation of CLDN18 IHC is that guidelines defining 1+ vs. 2+ membranous staining are not well described.

evidence.claim: Clinical trial eligibility criterion for zolbetuximab-based therapy required CLDN18 moderate-to-strong membranous expression by IHC in ≥75% of tumor cells, establishing the SPOTLIGHT and GLOW trial standard for surface-accessible CLDN18.2. This criterion implies that membranous (surface) IHC positivity was the therapeutic biomarker endpoint.

spans.quote: Eligible patients had tumors expressing CLDN18, with moderate to strong membranous expression by immunohistochemistry (IHC) in ≥75 % tumor cells[ 16 , 17 ].

evidence.claim: IHC scoring rule for CLDN18: only membranous staining was considered positive, explicitly excluding cytoplasmic staining from positivity calls. This is a critical methodological criterion ensuring that surface (membrane)-localized CLDN18 is distinguished from intracellular compartments in IHC assessment.

spans.quote: Only membranous staining was considered positive.

evidence.claim: IHC scoring system for CLDN18 membranous staining percentage: score 0 = 0%, 1+ = 1–4%, 2+ = 5–9%, 3+ = 10–24%, 4+ = 25–74%, 5+ = ≥75% of tumor cells with any membranous staining. This quantitative IHC rubric frames the surface-detection method for CLDN18 in a clinical cohort study.

spans.quote: The percentage of tumour cells with any membranous staining for CLDN18 was scored as follows: score 0, 0%; score 1+, 1%–4%; score 2+, 5%–9%; score 3+, 10%–24%; score 4+, 25%–74%; score 5+, ≥75%.

evidence.claim: Ovarian tumor surfaceome analysis identified CLDN18 among adhesion proteins with extracellular antigenic epitopes showing highest differential expression between tumor and normal tissues, alongside CLDN1 and EpCAM. This positions CLDN18 as having an accessible extracellular epitope for antibody-based targeting in the context of tumor surface antigen profiling.

spans.quote: The final set of genes encoding extracellular antigenic epitopes with the highest difference between ovarian tumors and normal tissues consisted of membrane receptors (TFRC, OPRK1, EBAG9, HTR5A, GABRB1, and CD47), adhesion proteins (EpCAM, CLDN18, and CLDN1), and molecule transporters (KCNG1, KCNH2, and SLC22A11) ( Figure 1E ).

evidence.claim: Cell-surface gene list for analysis was derived from the Cell Surface Protein Atlas (CSPA), which uses mass spectrometry data to define surface-localized proteins. CLDN18 presence in this resource is used as a surface-localization criterion, representing a database annotation of surface accessibility.

spans.quote: Cell surface genes considered for analysis were taken from lists derived from predictions from mass spectrometry data (Cell Surface Protein Atlas 103 ).

evidence.claim: Topology/localization table listing CLDN18 as a 'Membrane'-localized protein with tumor-to-normal differential expression (64.4 tumor, 133.3 normal, ratio 2.1) in a PDAC imaging target evaluation. CLDN18 is classified in the membrane compartment alongside other surface targets, supporting its surface localization designation.

spans.quote: crystal structure CEACAM5 Membrane 353.2 257.8 0.7 ND ND Low Yes MUCL3 Membrane/cytoplasm 152.0 57.8 0.4 ND ND ND No TMPRSS4 Membrane/intracellular 131.0 72.1 0.6 Medium Medium Low No COL17A1 Cell junction, desmosome, membrane 96.8 78.4 0.8 ND ND ND No MSLN Membrane 67.5 272.7 4.0 ND ND ND No CLDN18 Membrane 64.4 133.3 2.1 ND ND ND No PSCA Membrane 51.8 135.2 2.6 Low ND ND No AQP5 Membrane, cytoplasmic vesicle membrane 37.9 68.9 1.8 Low Low Low Yes VSIG2 Membrane 18.9 159.0 8.4 High High Medium No FXYD3 Membrane 17.4 236.8 13.6 ND Medium ND No GJB3 Membrane

evidence.claim: CLDN18.2 is described as a tight-junction protein overexpressed and 'differently exposed' in solid tumors including gastric cancer, gastroesophageal junction adenocarcinoma, and PDAC. The 'differently exposed' phrasing directly refers to enhanced cell-surface accessibility of the extracellular loop in tumor vs. normal epithelium, which is the mechanistic basis for therapeutic targeting.

spans.quote: Claudin 18.2 (CLDN18.2) is a tight-junction protein overexpressed and differently exposed in solid tumors such as gastric cancer (GCa), gastroesophageal junction adenocarcinoma, and pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (PDAC).

evidence.claim: 89Zr-labeled zolbetuximab (anti-CLDN18.2 monoclonal antibody) PET imaging in vivo: serial imaging at days 1, 3, and 6 post-injection in xenograft models confirmed specific tumor uptake, with [89Zr]Zr-DFO-IgG as negative control. This in vivo antibody-binding assay demonstrates that zolbetuximab engages CLDN18.2 on the surface of tumor cells accessible to circulating antibody.

spans.quote: Serial PET imaging with <sup>89</sup>Zr-labeled zolbetuximab, an anti-CLDN18.2 monoclonal antibody ([<sup>89</sup>Zr]Zr-DFO-zolbetuximab), and human IgG ([<sup>89</sup>Zr]Zr-DFO-IgG) as control (5.5-7.4 MBq) was performed 1, 3, and 6 d after injection, followed by ex vivo analysis of biodistribution.

evidence.claim: Serial PET imaging showed high tumor uptake of [89Zr]Zr-DFO-zolbetuximab at all 3 time points (day 6 mean: 24.4±7.8 %ID/g in GSU; 36.6±10.1 %ID/g in PATU8988S; 16.48±4.7 %ID/g in HUPT-4), significantly higher than [89Zr]Zr-DFO-IgG control (4.8±1.9 %ID/g in GSU, P=0.0029). This quantitative PET data demonstrates specific in vivo surface engagement of CLDN18.2 by zolbetuximab in PDAC and gastric cancer xenograft models, supporting therapeutic targeting of the extracellular epitope.

spans.quote: Serial PET imaging demonstrated a high tumor uptake of [89Zr]Zr-DFO-zolbetuximab at all 3 time points (mean uptake on day 6: 24.4 ± 7.8 %ID/g in GSU; 36.6 ± 10.1 %ID/g in PATU8988S; 16.48 ± 4.7 %ID/g in HUPT-4), and significantly higher than the uptake seen in mice imaged with [89Zr]Zr-DFO-IgG (day 6: 4.8 ± 1.9 %ID/g in GSU; P = 0.0029).

evidence.claim: RNA sequencing confirmed significantly higher expression of CLDN18.2 in human PDAC compared with noncancerous pancreas tissue, with no significant difference between treated and untreated tumors. This RNA-level observation supports differential CLDN18.2 expression in tumor tissue but does not directly confirm surface accessibility; it qualifies surface claims by showing transcriptional upregulation.

spans.quote: Toxicity of [177Lu]Lu-DOTA-zolbetuximab was evaluated by laboratory and histologic analyses over 90 d. Results: RNA sequencing confirmed significantly higher expression of CLDN18.2 in human PDAC compared with noncancerous pancreas tissue, with no significant difference between treated and untreated tumors.

evidence.claim: CLDN18.2-targeted CAR-T cell therapies have shown promising clinical efficacy in gastric cancer in early-phase trials. This therapeutic engagement claim demonstrates that CLDN18.2 is a clinically validated surface antigen accessible to engineered T cell receptors on tumor cells.

spans.quote: Claudin 18.2 (CLDN18.2)-targeted CAR-T cell therapies have shown promising clinical efficacy in gastric cancer.

evidence.claim: Early-phase CAR-T trials for CLDN18.2 in gastric cancer reported gastrointestinal adverse events due to on-target off-tumor recognition of CLDN18.2 in the gastric mucosa, confirming that CLDN18.2 is surface-accessible in normal gastric epithelium as well as tumor tissue. This on-target toxicity finding validates surface exposure of the antigen in non-malignant tissue.

spans.quote: However, early-phase trials have reported gastrointestinal adverse events due to on-target off-tumor recognition of CLDN18.2 in the gastric mucosa.

evidence.claim: Clinical antibodies (zolbetuximab) and CAR-T therapies targeting CLDN18.2 require cell-surface expression of CLDN18 for efficacy. In the context of CLDN18-ARHGAP26 fusion proteins, additional localization studies are needed to confirm surface exposure, highlighting that therapeutic engagement is contingent on plasma-membrane accessibility of the CLDN18 extracellular domain. This discussion note represents a surface-localization caveat for fusion gene contexts.

spans.quote: Clinically, CLDN18 protein has been an attractive target due to CLDN18.2 targeting antibodies demonstrating efficiency in early-phase clinical trials such as the monoclonal antibody zolbetuximab 29 as well as emerging chimeric antigen receptor-T cell (CAR-T) therapies. 30 However, in the context of CLDN18-ARHGAP26 fusion protein, additional studies will be needed to identify the exact localisation of the fusion gene in the cell and the suitability of therapies that require cell surface expression of CLDN18 for efficacy.

evidence.claim: Claudin-18 mRNA is expressed in lung tissue and is specifically down-regulated in T/ebp/Nkx2.1-null mouse embryo lungs, establishing the lung as a primary site of Claudin-18 expression under transcriptional control of NKX2.1.

spans.quote: Here we describe the isolation and characterization of a novel target gene, termed claudin-18, that is down-regulated in the lungs of T/ebp/Nkx2.1-null mouse embryos.

evidence.claim: CLDN18 produces two alternatively spliced isoforms driven by distinct promoters: a lung-specific transcript (CLDN18.1) and a stomach-specific transcript (CLDN18.2), restricting expression to these two tissue types at the mRNA level.

spans.quote: Alternative usage of these promoters leads to production of lung and stomach-specific transcripts.

evidence.claim: Among 11 claudin family members examined, only CLDN18 (claudin-18) mRNA is selectively down-regulated in NKX2.1-null embryo lungs, underscoring its unique lung-restricted expression pattern among claudins.

spans.quote: Only claudin-18 was down-regulated in T/ebp/Nkx2.1-null embryo lungs among 11 claudin transcripts examined.

evidence.claim: Claudin-18 protein constitutes tight junction strands in epithelial cells, placing it at the cell-cell contact surface subdomain (tight junctions) in epithelial tissues.

spans.quote: The gene product exhibits an amino acid sequence similar to those of the claudin multigene family of proteins that constitute tight junction strands in epithelial cells.

evidence.claim: HPA database annotates CLDN18 subcellular localization to cell junctions, consistent with tight junction membrane subdomain assignment.

spans.quote: HPA subcellular locations: Cell Junctions.

evidence.claim: HPA flags CLDN18 as a junctional protein, reinforcing localization to the cell-cell junction membrane subdomain and indicating restricted anatomical accessibility at junctional surfaces.

spans.quote: HPA flags this gene as junctional.

evidence.claim: CLDN18.2 (isoform 2) is a highly selective gastrocyte lineage-specific cell surface molecule, making it a lineage marker expressed at the surface of gastric epithelial cells.

spans.quote:
The purpose of this study was to discover novel targets for therapeutic antibodies in solid cancer.

Experimental design

We combined data mining and wet-bench experiments to identify strictly gastrocyte lineage-specific cell surface molecules and to validate them as therapeutic antibody targets.

Results

We identified isoform 2 of the tight junction molecule claudin-18 (CLDN18.2) as a highly selective cell lineage marker.

evidence.claim: CLDN18.2 expression in normal tissue is strictly confined to differentiated epithelial cells of the gastric mucosa and is absent from the gastric stem cell zone, establishing a cell-state-dependent surface expression pattern: present on differentiated gastric epithelium, absent on gastric progenitor/stem cells.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: gastric mucosa (differentiated epithelium vs. stem cell zone)

spans.quote: Its expression in normal tissues is strictly confined to differentiated epithelial cells of the gastric mucosa, but it is absent from the gastric stem cell zone.

evidence.claim: CLDN18.2 surface expression is retained upon malignant transformation and is present in a significant proportion of primary gastric cancers and their metastases, indicating accessibility of CLDN18.2 in gastric cancer tumor tissue.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: primary gastric cancer and metastatic samples

spans.quote: CLDN18.2 is retained on malignant transformation and is expressed in a significant proportion of primary gastric cancers and the metastases thereof.

evidence.claim: Beyond the stomach, CLDN18.2 shows frequent ectopic activation in pancreatic, esophageal, ovarian, and lung tumors correlating with distinct histological subtypes, indicating disease-state-induced surface expression in multiple tumor types not normally expressing this isoform.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: pancreatic, esophageal, ovarian, and lung tumor samples

spans.quote: In addition to its orthotopic expression, we found frequent ectopic activation of CLDN18.2 in pancreatic, esophageal, ovarian, and lung tumors, correlating with distinct histologic subtypes.

evidence.claim: CLDN18.1, the lung-specific isoform of Claudin-18, is normally expressed in alveolar epithelium as a tight junction protein, establishing this cell type and tissue compartment as the primary site of CLDN18.1 expression.

spans.quote: Claudin-18 (Cldn18), a tight junction protein with a lung-specific isoform (Cldn18.1), is normally expressed in alveolar epithelium and is implicated in maintaining alveolar integrity.

evidence.claim: Normal alveoli and reactive pneumocytes consistently express Cldn18, whereas lung adenocarcinomas (LUADs), especially non-mucinous LUADs (NM-LUADs), show marked loss of Cldn18 expression — indicating reduced CLDN18 surface accessibility in NM-LUAD tumor tissue relative to normal lung.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: normal alveolar epithelium vs. NM-LUAD tumor

spans.quote: Normal alveoli and reactive pneumocytes consistently expressed Cldn18, whereas LUADs, especially non-mucinous LUADs (NM-LUADs), typically showed marked loss.

evidence.claim: Complete absence of Cldn18 protein was observed in 83% of NM-LUAD resections and 84.8% of NM-LUAD biopsies, demonstrating near-complete loss of CLDN18 surface accessibility in non-mucinous lung adenocarcinoma — a contradictory observation relative to reports of CLDN18 ectopic activation in lung tumors (which likely refers to CLDN18.2 in mucinous subtypes).

spans.quote: Complete absence of Cldn18 was observed in 83% of NM-LUAD resections and 84.8% of NM-LUAD biopsies.

evidence.claim: Lepidic NM-LUAD components show low Cldn18 expression in more than 50% of tumor cells in 24 of 26 cases examined, confirming near-absent CLDN18 in this morphological subtype of NM-LUAD despite adjacent normal lung expressing it robustly.

spans.quote: Lepidic NM-LUAD components, a frequent diagnostic pitfall especially in small specimens, showed low expression in most cases (>50% of tumour cells in 24/26).

evidence.claim: IHC across a pan-cancer cohort detected CLDN18.2 positivity (≥40% of tumor cells, any staining intensity) in 16.3% of patients overall, with highest prevalence in gastric cancer (54.5%), biliary tract cancer (21.7%), pancreatic cancer (20.7%), and small intestinal cancer (18.2%), establishing tissue-specific expression frequencies for CLDN18.2 across solid tumor types.

cell_context.disease_state: cancer (gastric, biliary tract, pancreatic, small intestinal)

spans.quote: IHC detected CLDN18.2 (≥ 40% of tumor cells showing any staining intensity) in 16.3% of patients, with highest prevalence in gastric (54.5%), biliary tract (21.7%), pancreatic (20.7%), and small intestinal (18.2%) cancers.

evidence.claim: Whole-transcriptome sequencing (WTS) across 2,191 solid tumor samples identified a CLDN18-high population in 13.8% of patients, with highest proportions in gastric cancer (64.5%), small intestinal cancer (40.0%), pancreatic cancer (37.8%), and biliary tract cancer (20.0%), confirming mRNA-level enrichment in gastrointestinal tumor lineages.

cell_context.disease_state: cancer (gastric, small intestinal, pancreatic, biliary tract)

spans.quote: WTS analysis with optimized transcript thresholds (n = 2191) demonstrated the CLDN18-high population to be 13.8%, with highest proportions in gastric (64.5%), small intestinal (40.0%), pancreatic (37.8%), and biliary tract (20.0%) cancers.

evidence.claim: Isoform analysis across 364 solid tumor patients reveals strong predominance of CLDN18.2 over CLDN18.1 (mean CLDN18.2/CLDN18.1 proportion 0.945), with CLDN18.1 predominance in only 4.9% of cases, indicating that tumor-expressed CLDN18 is almost entirely the CLDN18.2 isoform.

spans.quote: Isoform analysis of 364 patients revealed CLDN18.2 predominance (mean 18.2/18.1 proportion 0.945), with CLDN18.1 predominance observed in only 4.9% of patients.

evidence.claim: IHC across multiple cancer types reveals CLDN18.2 expression in 68% (27/40) of lung mucinous adenocarcinomas, 52% (16/31) of cholangiocarcinomas, and only 2% (10/423) of colorectal adenocarcinomas, establishing lung mucinous adenocarcinoma and cholangiocarcinoma as tumor types with meaningful CLDN18.2 surface accessibility.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: lung mucinous adenocarcinoma, cholangiocarcinoma, colorectal adenocarcinoma tumor sections

cell_context.disease_state: cancer (lung mucinous adenocarcinoma, cholangiocarcinoma, colorectal adenocarcinoma)

spans.quote:
Aims

To clarify claudin18.2 expression and its clinicopathological features in various cancers, especially in lung adenocarcinoma.

Methods

Immunohistochemistry staining and fluorescence in situ hybridisation (FISH) were performed to detect claudin18.2 expression and CLDN18 gene rearrangement in adenocarcinoma from different organs.

Results

The results showed that claudin18.2 expression was found in 68% (27 of 40) of lung mucinous adenocarcinoma, 52% (16 of 31) of cholangiocarcinoma, 2% (10 of 423) of colorectal adenocarcinoma tissue microarray

evidence.claim: CLDN18.2 expression is detected by IHC in biliary precursor lesions including intraductal papillary neoplasm of the bile duct and epithelial dysplasia adjacent to cholangiocarcinoma, indicating early-stage disease-context expression before frank malignancy.

spans.quote: Intraductal papillary neoplasm of the bile duct and epithelial dysplasia of the adjacent bile in cholangiocarcinoma also showed claudin18.2 expression.

evidence.claim: CLDN18.2 expression is detected by IHC in cervical adenocarcinoma cases, specifically in moderately differentiated gastric-type endocervical adenocarcinomas (including both HPV-associated and non-HPV-associated subtypes), suggesting ectopic CLDN18.2 expression linked to gastric differentiation phenotype in cervical tumors.

spans.quote:
All three cases of cervical adenocarcinoma with claudin18.2 expression were moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma including one human papillomavirus (HPV)-associated carcinoma, two non-HPV-associated and gastric-type carcinoma. CLDN18 gene rearrangement was not found in all 22 cases with high claudin18.2 expression by FISH.

Conclusions

Our results suggest claudin18.2 might be a potential biomarker for targeted therapy on lung mucinous adenocarcinoma, cholangiocarcinoma, colorectal mucinous adenocarcinoma and gastric-type cervical adenocarcinoma.

evidence.claim: In a cohort of 237 biliary tract cancer (BTC) samples, CLDN18 protein expression was observed in 29.5% (70/237) and strict CLDN18 positivity (moderate-to-strong membranous expression in ≥75% of tumor cells) was found in only 5.5% (13/237), indicating that biliary tract cancers show moderate CLDN18.2 surface accessibility at the tumor cell membrane but at lower frequency than gastric cancer.

spans.quote: Overall, CLDN18 expression was observed in 70/237 BTCs (29.5%) samples, while CLDN18 positivity (i.e. moderate-to-strong membranous expression in ≥75% of the tumor cells) was found in 13/237 BTCs (5.5%).

evidence.claim: Proteomic/surfaceome data assign CLDN18 to membrane localization in PDAC tumor analysis, with a tumor-to-normal expression ratio of 2.1 (133.3 vs 64.4 arbitrary units), indicating moderate enrichment of CLDN18 at the cancer cell membrane relative to normal pancreatic tissue.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: PDAC tumor vs normal pancreatic tissue

evidence.claim: Protein expression of CLDN18 was compared across PDAC, chronic pancreatitis (CP), and normal pancreatic, duodenal, and liver tissue sections by IHC, providing multi-tissue disease-vs-normal profiling for CLDN18 accessibility in the pancreatic disease context.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: PDAC, chronic pancreatitis, normal pancreas, duodenum, liver sections

spans.quote: The protein expressions of CEACAM5, TMPRSS4, COL17A1, CLDN18, and AQP5 in representative PDAC, CP, pancreatic, duodenal, and liver tissue sections are shown in Figure 1 .

evidence.claim: Integrated transcriptomic and proteomic surfaceome investigation of PDAC identifies CLDN18 as an established therapeutic target with low or minimal expression in most normal toxicity-prone tissues, supporting selective tumor surface accessibility of CLDN18 in pancreatic cancer.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: PDAC tumor and normal tissue panel

spans.quote: Transcriptional and proteomic investigation of the cell surfaceome revealed established therapeutic targets ( CLDN18 , MSLN , TROP2 , MUC1 , CEACAM5 ) and discovered additional PDAC antigens ( GPRC5A , TM4SF1 ) with low or minimal expression in most toxicity-prone normal tissues.

evidence.claim: Computational analysis of transcriptomic data identifies CLDN18 as one of the genes encoding extracellular antigenic epitopes with highest differential expression between ovarian tumors and normal tissues, classifying it as an adhesion protein with tumor-enriched surface accessibility in ovarian cancer.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: ovarian tumor vs. normal tissue transcriptomic dataset

cell_context.disease_state: ovarian cancer vs. normal ovarian tissue

spans.quote: The final set of genes encoding extracellular antigenic epitopes with the highest difference between ovarian tumors and normal tissues consisted of membrane receptors (TFRC, OPRK1, EBAG9, HTR5A, GABRB1, and CD47), adhesion proteins (EpCAM, CLDN18, and CLDN1), and molecule transporters (KCNG1, KCNH2, and SLC22A11) ( Figure 1E ).

evidence.claim: An independent study corroborated that CLDN18 expression is almost exclusively restricted to pancreatic, esophageal, ovarian, and lung cancer tissues among solid tumors, reinforcing the tissue-lineage selectivity of CLDN18.2 ectopic expression.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: pancreatic, esophageal, ovarian, lung cancer tissues

spans.quote: Another study corroborated that CLDN18 was expressed almost exclusively in pancreatic, esophageal, ovarian, and lung cancer tissues ( Sahin et al., 2008 ).

evidence.claim: Clinical trial eligibility for PDAC patients requires moderate-to-strong membranous CLDN18 expression by IHC in ≥75% of tumor cells, confirming membranous (cell surface) CLDN18 localization in a subset of PDAC tumors as a clinically validated surface accessibility criterion.

spans.quote: Eligible patients had tumors expressing CLDN18, with moderate to strong membranous expression by immunohistochemistry (IHC) in ≥75 % tumor cells[ 16 , 17 ].

evidence.claim: In the context of CLDN18-ARHGAP26 fusion proteins (occurring in some gastric tumors), the exact cellular localization of the fusion protein is uncertain, raising the question of whether sufficient CLDN18 surface expression is retained for antibody- or CAR-T-based therapies that depend on cell-surface accessibility of CLDN18.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: gastric cancer tumor cells with CLDN18-ARHGAP26 fusion

spans.quote: Clinically, CLDN18 protein has been an attractive target due to CLDN18.2 targeting antibodies demonstrating efficiency in early-phase clinical trials such as the monoclonal antibody zolbetuximab 29 as well as emerging chimeric antigen receptor-T cell (CAR-T) therapies. 30 However, in the context of CLDN18-ARHGAP26 fusion protein, additional studies will be needed to identify the exact localisation of the fusion gene in the cell and the suitability of therapies that require cell surface expression of CLDN18 for efficacy.

evidence.claim: CLDN18 protein is frequently co-expressed in CD-SBN lesions (intestinal metaplasia in Barrett's context) showing membranous localization, often co-expressed with gastric-type mucins MUC5AC and MUC6, indicating CLDN18 surface expression in specialized intestinal metaplasia / gastric-type differentiation of the esophagus.

spans.quote: Interestingly, we found 92% of CD-SBNs were CDH17 positive and 91% of them showed ≥ 40% extent with moderate or strong membranous expression, and approximately half of them co-expressed gastric-type mucins, such as MUC5AC and MUC6 with frequently CLDN18.

evidence.claim: Surfaceome and proteomic profiling of adenocarcinoma of the esophagogastric junction (AEG) tumor samples compared to paired normal adjacent tissue (NAT) shows log2 fold-change enrichment of CLDN18 in AEG tumor tissue, establishing CLDN18 as a differentially expressed surface protein in GEJ cancer.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: AEG tumor vs paired normal adjacent tissue (GEJ patients)

spans.quote: A Heatmap of top 20 DEPs between AEG tumor and paired NAT samples; B Venn diagram of top 20 DEPs and membrane proteins from Cancer Surfaceome Atlas; C heatmap of the normal tissue expression index of eight candidate targets; D bar chart of log 2 fold-change of established ADC targets (HER2, TROP2, and CLDN18) and CD66c in the tumor and precancerous tissues in each GEJ patient; E heatmap of clinicopathological characteristics among different CD66c expression level; F box plots compare CD66c protein expression quantities in different Siewert types

confidence_reasoning: A1 evidence_grade is direct_multi_method with zero contradicting_evidence entries. A2 confirms moderate state_dependence (junctional restriction relieved in tumors; CLDN18.2 lost in NM-LUAD but retained/ectopically activated in gastric, PDAC, biliary, mucinous lung, and ovarian tumors). The integrated evidence base includes an approved drug (zolbetuximab), quantitative in vivo PET imaging, live-cell FACS with isoform-selective reagents, and clinical CAR-T toxicity data — all independently confirming surface-accessible extracellular epitopes. No secreted/shed form and no co-receptor dependency further support high confidence. State dependence is moderate rather than high because the tumor-context accessibility shift is well-characterized and the junctional restriction does not preclude systemic antibody engagement as demonstrated clinically.
